Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Hold on to your sewing chairs! The Midnight Quilt Show is going bold, as in late-night foundation paper-piecing bold. (Angela makes it easy!)
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: First, Dave Munkittrick demonstrates a handy tip for creating easy-to-use sanding pads. Then, George Vondriska and Matt Newborg discuss how to achieve a high-gloss finish on your woodworking projects. Learn to prevent over-sanding and troubleshoot imperfections in your wood surface with their expert tips.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Achieving a High-Gloss Finish

Video.summary: George Vondriska shows you how to build your own backyard barbeque cart for your next cookout. Learn the best types of wood to use and how to assemble your cart to achieve a professional look. George also discusses which glue and fasteners to use for a barbeque cart that will withstand the elements.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Backyard Barbecue Cart

Video.summary: Learn the ins and outs of glue with George Vondriska as he explains the differences between adhesives and when to use them in the shop. Find out which glues are best for your woodworking projects and which glues to avoid, then see how George recommends spreading your glue for the best consistency and clamping your materials to ensure proper bonding.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Gluing and Clamping Tips

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Angela is back for Season 5 of the Midnight Quilt Show! Today she is making the Arrows Quilt by Krystal Jakelwicz, and incorporating a little love note into her machine quilting.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Good things come in small packages. Create a mini book from a 3D kaleidoscope with Joe Rotella, a mini journal with Jenny Barnett Rohrs, and 3D sculptured surface for your journal with Kim Hupke. All today on Scrapbook Soup.
Video.title: Mini Books

Video.summary: Look at quilting from a fresh angle. Quilt designer and author Latifah Saafir discusses how to design with half-rectangle shapes. Heather Grant and Elizabeth Dackson give tips for creating a guild demo day. Painter-turned-quilter Victoria Findlay Wolfe plays with color patterns to create compelling designs with die-cut shapes.
Video.title: A New Angle

Video.summary: Angela shares her tips for choosing quick and impactful quilting designs for your pieced creations.
Video.title: Bonus: Tips for Picking Quilt Designs

Image.alt: Bonus: Tips for Picking Quilt Designs
Bonus: Tips for Picking Quilt Designs
Bonus: Tips for Picking Quilt Designs

Video.summary: Can’t get enough of bargello? Angela Walters shows you how to make a ravishing rainbow quilt using precut fabric strip sets.
Video.title: Bargello Beauty Rainbow Quilt

Video.summary: Angela Walters reminisces about her days in the Girl Scouts while making the Campfire Quilt designed by the one and only Suzy Williams of Suzy Quilts.
Video.title: Campfire Quilt

Video.summary: This week's quilt radiates piece and love. Angela uses color placement for a heart-shaped quilt that's easier to make than it looks. IMPORTANT NOTE: The "Piece & Love" quilt designed by Audrey Mann and Dianne Brinton is no longer included with the class.
Video.title: Piece & Love Quilt

Video.summary: Angela creates a modern Southwestern-style quilt that's the perfect size to use as a blanket for summer picnics in the park.
Video.title: Modern Southwestern-Style Quilt

Video.summary: Sometimes bulky seams can't be avoided, but DON'T panic! Angela has a few tips for how to deal with this quilting conundrum.
Video.title: Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams

Image.alt: Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams
Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams
Bonus: Tips for Avoiding Bulky Seams

Video.summary: Quilting just got better. Join Angela in her first episode as she stitches up her exclusive variable-star quilt design.
Video.title: Stars

Video.summary: Quilting just got better. Join Angela in her first episode as she stitches up her exclusive variable-star quilt design.
Video.title: Stars

Video.summary: It was only a matter of time before the Midnight Quilt Show tackled Janice Ryan's Wallflower Quilt! You bring the wine, Angela's got the Flying Geese covered.
Video.title: Wallflower Variable Star Quilt

Video.summary: Angela ponders on her new year's resolutions as she builds a curved log cabin quilt using ombré fabric.
Video.title: Curved Log Cabin Quilt

Video.summary: Do you experience stash-guilt? Well, Angela's got the cure with this scrappy stash-busting pattern.
Video.title: A Scrappy Stash-Buster Quilt Pattern

Video.summary: Our experts show some of their insider tips for garment construction. First is sewing expert Linda Lee with a lesson on sewing with knits and pucker free hems. Angela Wolf is next with a Little Black Dress segment and how to deal with zippers that are just too long for your garment. Finally, Cindy Hogan demonstrates the way the professionals cut machine embroidered designs for applique.
Video.title: Tutorials on the Classics

Video.summary: We expand our view of classics by adding surface embellishments to personalize your garment. Cindy Hogan makes appliques from embroidery to add detail to any garment. Then, it's our next lesson in the Little Black Dress from Angela Wolf and different seam finishes including the Hong Kong. Last is embroidery expert Eileen Roche and easy dress embroidery for beautiful style.
Video.title: Dress Up Your Wardrobe

Video.summary: Author and designer Kristal Wick has some fabulous stringing projects featuring double drilled beads. She shows how to design coordinating necklace and bracelet using double drilled beads. Kristal also has a segment discussing metal clay. Katie shows how to store and organize seed beads.
Video.title: Stringing Projects Using Double-drilled Beads

Video.summary: Lauren Andersen, The Chainmaille Lady has a new take on mixing other materials with chainmaille, including needle tatting and premade cuffs. Lauren also shares a tip for making your own jump rings. Katie makes a beaded cluster bracelet in her Beading Lesson.
Video.title: Needle Tatting and Chainmaille

Video.summary: Bulk up your jewelry design skills. Designer Susan Lenart warms up and works out by adding tints, glitter, graphics and bits of nature to layers of resin. She adds feathers, leather, threads and wires to create organic jewelry designs. Ana Piliguian cools downs by polishing resin elements to create high-gloss finishes.
Video.title: Bodybuilding with Resin

Video.summary: It's the little things that count – from the tiniest embellishment to the handwritten letter - it's all in the details today on Scrapbook Soup. Create simple cut out graphic cards, learn about packing tape photo transfer with Sara Naumann, and backgrounds almost as pretty as the picture in a prom layout with Stacey Caron– today on Scrapbook Soup.
Video.title: It's All in the Details

Video.summary: Plant life is a key element of the world around us and one we bring indoors too. Today's show starts with host Julie Fei Fan Balzer and a short tutorial about shades of green, a featured color of plants. Then, Cat Kerr creates a unique art piece featuring a face that is highlighted by flowers. Next, Lynne Suprock creates a honey comb pot to hold your treasured plants. We finish with a sketchbook lesson with Tori Weyers and seeds and pods; they hold the potential to be new plant life and can be a source of inspiration.
Video.title: Episode 12: Plants

Video.summary: Time for a little spring fling! Join the Grocery Girls as they gear up for warmer weather by chatting lace, their favorite Instagrammers and even a few tales of love. Well, sort of.
Video.title: Fresh for Spring: Knitted Boot Liners, Jewel-tone Shawls & Brioche Police

Image.alt: Fresh for Spring: Knitted Boot Liners, Jewel-tone Shawls & Brioche Police
Fresh for Spring: Knitted Boot Liners, Jewel-tone Shawls & Brioche Police
Fresh for Spring: Knitted Boot Liners, Jewel-tone Shawls & Brioche Police

Video.summary: Since when do knitters use crochet hooks? Find out how to work the provisional cast-on, cable cast-on, and more during the first Novel-T check in!
Video.title: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Image.alt: Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline
Provisional Cast-On, Cable Cast-On & Split Neckline

Video.summary: One of the keys of impressionism is the blending together of images. The definition of this movement in art is characterized by visible brush strokes, ordinary subject matters, and an emphasis on light and its changing qualities. The first project is an impressionistic landscape using oil pastels and black mat board. Then we're using dye in spray bottles to make colorful geometric designs. Learn about color blending using special tools to create interesting effects in your art journal. And finally, blend colored glue to create a piece of art.
Video.title: Blending

Video.summary: Let's get creative with inspiration from the forest, sea and sky. Jenny Barnett-Rohrs and Katie Hacker make a friendly hedgehog then Katie creates a wiggly fish. Franz Spohn scratches off paint to make colorful animal images and Jenny makes a gelatin print Valentine's Day card.
Video.title: Fin, Feathers and Fur

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
